visible_observations: Twelve master cards appear in a three-by-four grid with a common smoky dark background, low-chroma brown-black palette, isolated full-body framing, and a silhouette strip beneath each master.
Veyra, Nyxa, Creep, and Malachor retain strongly nonhuman outer shapes; Korath's arch, Pilfer's hand, Kett's fist, Sable's rifle, and Knoll's paper-and-stamp action provide other large identifiers.
Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, and Sable share tall human proportions, fitted dark clothing, narrow waists, long legs, and mostly restrained studio poses.
defects: Dark leather, black cloth, brown straps, smoky backgrounds, and similar low-key lighting recur across most humanoids, compressing garment and material separation.
Cashmere and Quillith both combine an attractive standing woman, a rectangular occult document object, fitted dark trousers, and an extended hand.
Image scale and empty margins vary substantially between tall humanoids, wide creatures, and compact units, weakening direct size comparison.
uncertainties: The sheet does not show actual gameplay spacing, neighboring placement, animation, or environment.
The card layout does not establish whether apparent scale differences are character scale or image fitting.
contamination: Every card prints the unit name, role, trait labels, and a selected-silhouette letter beside the visual evidence.

visible_observations: Twelve face or attention crops appear in a two-column board at similar card widths, making eye direction, mouth shape, head angle, and nonhuman attention mechanisms visible together.
Korath is downcast and compressed, Pilfer smiles crookedly, Kett exposes teeth, Sable tracks sideways, and Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna use mild narrowed-eye or small-smile expressions.
Veyra and Malachor show enclosed anatomy or an organ cage rather than a conventional face, while Creep shows black eye cavities without a mouth.
defects: Several attractive humanoid faces converge on polished skin, controlled brows, direct or sideways eyes, and restrained closed mouths rather than strongly differentiated visible states.
Veyra and Malachor lack a clear gaze or facial action in their crops, so their attention cannot be compared on the same visual terms as the humanoids.
Crop scale and included body context differ, with some cards showing a tight head and others showing large anatomy or weapon regions.
uncertainties: The still board cannot reveal blinking, pulsing organs, shifting apertures, or other temporal attention cues.
contamination: Each crop is placed beside extensive answer-bearing text for private want, primary fear, active survival strategy, current emotion, contradiction, visible face direction, and forbidden read.
The board header states the intended review question and an explicit stopping condition before the images are inspected.

visible_observations: Across the twelve crops, human and humanoid faces share the same dark studio background, close three-quarter framing, controlled realistic rendering, and subdued palette.
The roster spans bearded giant, pod anatomy, several adult women, ratlike humanoid, batlike creature, faceless alien, pointed-ear clerk, tattooed bruiser, and organ-cage monster.

Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, and Sable all retain attractive adult facial proportions, groomed dark or blonde hair, restrained lips, and composed eyes, while their surface markings and props provide most differentiation.
defects: The composed female expressions cluster around calm confidence, faint smile, or cool concentration, reducing emotional separation at roster scale.
Several male or humanoid faces also use a compressed brow plus closed or partly exposed mouth, creating a recurring serious-or-smug family.
uncertainties: The board does not show the faces at gameplay scale or in moving combat states.
contamination: Names, roles, sex categories, deliberate-horror labels, full psychology explanations, visible-direction instructions, and forbidden interpretations are printed next to every crop.

visible_observations: Twelve small square unit images appear in equal cards, allowing the native derivatives to be scanned in a consistent grid.
Veyra's pod, Nyxa's bow and ears, Creep's tendril ring, Malachor's pincer, Korath's arch, Kett's fist, and Sable's rifle remain the strongest small-scale shapes.
Cashmere, Knoll, Quillith, and Sable reduce to narrow dark humanoids whose prop or arm extension carries most recognition.
defects: Faces, skin markings, small supernatural effects, document surfaces, garment construction, and most material distinctions disappear across the set.
The common near-black backgrounds merge with dark clothing and rear appendages in many icons.
The printed text for Quillith and Malachor visibly truncates inside their cards, showing layout pressure around long trait strings.
uncertainties: The board does not show these icons on an actual battlefield, with selection states, health bars, overlap, or motion.
contamination: Every icon is accompanied by the unit name, role, traits, and a printed primary trait that can identify or explain an otherwise ambiguous image.

visible_observations: The nonhuman units remain separated by pod, bow, tendril, arch, and pincer silhouettes, while the humanoid cluster shares dark bodies on matching dark squares.
Cashmere and Quillith both read as standing women with one extended arm and one rectangular object; Knoll also retains a pale rectangular paper accent.
Kett's enlarged fist and Sable's long rifle preserve role-scale asymmetry more clearly than the smaller hand, face, and garment identifiers of the other humanoids.
defects: Shared dark palettes and similar upright human proportions narrow roster separation among Cashmere, Knoll, Quillith, Luna, and Sable.
Most individual psychology and supernatural-cost marks do not survive at this grid scale.
uncertainties: Names and role text remain adjacent to every image, so unaided icon-only roster recognition is not isolated by the board.
contamination: The unit names, roles, traits, and primary-trait labels provide answer-bearing identity beside each native-size image.

visible_observations: The twelve masters are grouped into Tank, Mage, Assassin, Support, Marksman, and Brawler panels, revealing large within-role differences for tanks, assassins, and marksmen.

Cashmere and Luna share slim adult female bodies, open-hand casting gestures, fitted dark clothing, and long legs; Knoll and Quillith both pair a dark humanoid with a pale rectangular document object.
Nyxa and Sable separate clearly through organic bow versus rifle, while Korath, Veyra, and Malachor separate through giant humanoid, pod, and arthropod construction.
defects: Wide creatures and tall humanoids are fitted into different image windows and empty margins, making body-scale and finish comparison uneven.
Some role groups contain only one unit or visually dissimilar mechanisms, so the board does not consistently expose the closest visual neighbors.
Dark fitted garments, straps, boots, and smoky backgrounds recur across most humanoids despite different role headings.
uncertainties: The board supplies no gameplay scene or interaction proving how the grouped silhouettes separate during use.
contamination: Every panel includes producer-authored lane, prop or anatomy, visible-cost, emotion, and contradiction text beside the master.
The header explicitly instructs what to compare and states that the grouped labels expose same-role collisions.

visible_observations: A large separate Vellum panel appears above a three-by-four grid of the twelve current masters; all use dark isolated character presentation.

The upper figure combines a long dark tailored coat, corseted torso, raised quill, and many curling paper strips; Cashmere, Knoll, and Quillith also use dark tailored garments with book, paper, board, or inscription motifs.
Nonhuman units preserve strong body-plan separation, while several humanoids repeat black or brown leather, long panels, straps, boots, and open-hand presentation.
defects: The upper reference panel and current-unit cards use different crop sizes, backgrounds, poses, and surrounding text, limiting direct visual comparison.
Paper or document motifs recur across the upper figure, Cashmere, Knoll, and Quillith, while fitted dark leather and long tailored panels recur more broadly.
The current masters are scaled to equal card widths rather than equal character height or use scale.
uncertainties: The board does not show how repeated materials separate under gameplay lighting or at native runtime size.
contamination: The upper panel explicitly labels Vellum as a reference, states its intended comparison function, lists what not to copy, asks an answer-bearing phase question, and prints approval-anchor provenance.
Each current card prints lane, emotion, and dominant-idea text beneath the image.

visible_observations: All twelve masters are visible together beneath the separate upper figure, exposing consistent dark backgrounds, detailed surface rendering, and isolated full-body presentation.
Individual master identities are carried by large dominant shapes: Korath's arch, Veyra's pod, Pilfer's hand, Nyxa's bow, Creep's tendrils, Kett's fist, Malachor's pincer, and Sable's rifle.
Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna depend more heavily on small skin marks, thin effects, hand gestures, and rectangular props than on a radically different body silhouette.
defects: Several current humanoids repeat fitted dark trousers, boots, straps, long panels, and studio-front presentation.
Fine identity mechanisms such as ink drips, skin lattices, copper cracks, facial scars, and document diagrams are much less visible at the board's master-card scale.
The current masters are not shown at a common physical or gameplay scale.
uncertainties: The board cannot establish rear-view construction, animation, material response, or interaction behavior for any master.
contamination: The file surrounds the masters with explicit reference instructions, intended lane and emotion labels, dominant-idea claims, and approval-source text.
